title: Celestial Memory Mechanism
description: A suspended astronomical instrument built from eight interlocking orbital frames, a dim central memory lens, and fractured constellation shards. It combines cathedral-scale engineering with the delicacy of a hand-held astrolabe. The object should appear older than any civilization in the realm while still actively calculating relationships among remembered events.
narrativeFunction: The mechanism visualizes memory as a celestial system. Stable recollections occupy durable orbits while unstable fragments drift between rings. It establishes upper-world scale, provides a slow-moving orientation landmark, and gives the sky a mechanical hierarchy visible from every authored camera path.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin is the exact center of the memory lens; every orbital rotation, suspension point, and attachment socket is measured from this focus.

silhouette.primary: A broad circular celestial crown made from several offset rings, wider than it is deep, with one visibly broken quadrant that prevents the asset from reading as a perfect target.
silhouette.secondary: Thin radial arms connect selected rings to a dark central lens while three eccentric counterweight arcs extend beyond the primary circumference and create a deliberately unbalanced clockwork profile.
silhouette.tertiary: Needle-like constellation markers, engraved calibration vanes, suspended shard cages, tiny luminous rivets, and exposed hinge plates concentrate detail near intersections rather than coating every surface uniformly.
silhouette.negativeSpace: Open spaces between orbital rings remain dominant. The central lens occupies less than one fifth of the frontal area, and the broken quadrant creates a memorable wedge of empty sky.
silhouette.recognitionTest: At thumbnail size the asset reads as an incomplete celestial instrument with several eccentric orbits and one dark eye, never as a flat halo, decorative target, or generic atom symbol.

materials.surfaceDescription: Dark aged brass with rubbed edges, shallow engraved calibrations, oxidation around rivets, and visible assembly scars where orbital sections were repaired.
Smoked violet glass with deep internal refraction, a cloudy core, and a bright narrow rim suggesting stored pressure beneath the surface.
Pale ceramic-metal fragments with chipped edges, sparse gold inlay, and emissive glyphs carved into their depth rather than printed onto the face.

components.role: Largest load-bearing orbit and dominant silhouette ring.
components.detail: The ring carries engraved degree marks, six hinge plates, uneven reinforcement, and one intentionally missing forty-degree segment with modeled mechanical termination ends.

components.role: Seven secondary orbital frames crossing at different non-symmetrical inclinations.
components.detail: Each ring varies slightly in thickness, tilt, and engraved index so the mechanism feels accumulated and repaired rather than manufactured in one pass.

components.role: Dark optical volume acting as the visual and animation pivot.
components.detail: The sphere is flattened into a thick lens with an uninterrupted front surface, a brighter edge, and enough depth to support animated internal refraction.

components.role: Mechanical spokes connecting selected orbital structures to the lens cage.
components.detail: Arms taper toward the lens and terminate in visible forked joints rather than disappearing into ring geometry; not every ring receives a spoke.

components.role: Faceted memory markers distributed throughout the outer orbital volume.
components.detail: Markers become thin irregular shards oriented toward nearby orbit intersections and clustered into readable constellations rather than uniform noise.

assemblyOrder: Connect selected rings with radial arms
Distribute constellation shards and calibration details
deformationRules: Flatten the central lens to thirty-eight percent depth while preserving a circular frontal profile.
Remove one forty-degree section from the primary orbit and bend adjacent ends outward by six degrees to expose the fracture.
Apply no more than two degrees of handcrafted warp to secondary rings so they feel ancient without appearing soft.
variationRules: Ring inclination may vary by twelve degrees, but no two rings may become coplanar.
Shard scale varies from sixty-five to one hundred fifty-five percent and clusters near orbital intersections.
Calibration engravings may change per instance while preserving the same density hierarchy.

topology.target: Game-ready hard-surface topology with editable modular ring sections, clean circular deformation, and continuous shading across hero-facing orbital surfaces.
topology.bevel: Use physically meaningful bevel widths on metal rings, arm forks, lens cage, and shard edges; avoid razor-thin highlights.
topology.subdivision: Reserve subdivision for the lens and selected ceremonial ring transitions; preserve intentionally faceted shards and calibration hardware.
topology.normals: Use weighted normals on metal structures so broad surfaces remain calm while bevels and engraved cuts carry highlights.
topology.uv: Use trim-sheet-aligned orbital UVs, unique lens UVs, and a dedicated emissive-mask channel for calibration glyphs and shard marks.

animation.motionCharacter: Extremely slow ceremonial movement with independent orbital rates. Nothing spins like a powered machine; it should feel as though gravity, memory, and time negotiate every rotation.
constraints: The broken orbit never intersects adjacent rings.
The lens remains fixed at the origin.
Shard drift amplitude remains below twelve centimeters.

collision.description: Use one thin toroidal proxy for the primary orbit and one flattened sphere for the lens; decorative rings, arms, and shards remain non-colliding.

sockets.purpose: World-space suspension or chain attachment above the mechanism.
Camera, light, or interaction focus at the center of the memory lens.
Particle source aligned with the broken orbital quadrant.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show the broad circular crown, broken upper-right quadrant, central dark lens, and eccentric counterweights clearly. At least five ring intersections remain visible without tangencies.
orthographicGuidance.side: Emphasize shallow total depth, layered orbital offsets, flattened lens volume, and staggered arm joints; the asset must not collapse into one thin line.
orthographicGuidance.top: Reveal the family of ring inclinations and selective spoke connections while preserving open passages among orbital layers.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Use the broken quadrant as the leading edge and show both lens depth and counterweight arcs extending beyond the main crown.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: Include a two-meter human silhouette beneath the mechanism; the main crown should appear approximately four human heights across.

validation: Primary silhouette reads as an incomplete multi-orbit astrolabe from fifty meters.
At least thirty-five percent of the frontal projection remains open negative space.
The central lens is the darkest contiguous shape and remains distinct from the frame.
No two secondary rings share identical inclination and motion rate.
Broken ring ends contain modeled mechanical termination details rather than raw open topology.
LOD2 preserves the broken quadrant, central lens, and three eccentric ring directions.
All sockets export with stable names and correct world orientation.
